_metadata.match: Houston Dynamo FC vs Louisville City FC
_metadata.competition: Lamar Hunt U.S. Open Cup — Round of 16 (111th edition)
_metadata.stakes: Winner advances to Quarterfinals (live draw on CBS Sports Golazo Network's Morning Footy on Thursday, April 30, at 8:00 a.m. CT). Tournament champion earns a 2027 Concacaf Champions Cup berth.
_metadata.vibe: USL royalty rolls into Houston after upsetting Austin. Reigning USLC champs, undefeated-ish, with a striker who can't stop scoring and the reigning USL POY pulling strings. We have OUR USL POY (Markanich, 2024). Cup soccer chaos potential: HIGH.

houston.all_comps_record: 4W-5L-0D MLS through Matchday 10 + 1W Open Cup (1-0 vs SD on 4/22, then 0-2 loss at Austin on 4/25)
houston.mls_position: 12 pts, 15th West (per Yahoo recap of Austin loss)
houston.recent_form_in_words: Won three straight in all comps then got bullied 2-0 at Q2 Stadium against Austin (Nelson + Uzuni goals). Three-game winning streak DEAD.
houston.stat_of_doom: Houston averages 42.5% possession in MLS — 29th out of 30 teams. Bend, don't break, but mostly bend.
houston.open_cup_pedigree: 2023 U.S. Open Cup CHAMPIONS under Olsen. Six players still on roster from that winning team: Artur, Héctor Herrera, Aliyu Ibrahim, Erik Sviatchenko (2023 squad), plus cup winners with other clubs Mateusz Bogusz (2024 LAFC) and Antônio Carlos (2022 Orlando).

louisville.all_comps_record: 5W-1L-1D in USL Championship (16 pts, 1st in Eastern Conference) + Open Cup wins over Southern Indiana FC (2-0) and Austin FC (2-1) + USL Cup wins including 3-1 over Fort Wayne
louisville.current_streak: THREE-GAME WINNING STREAK across all comps coming in
louisville.open_cup_run: R32: beat Southern Indiana FC 2-0 (Showunmi brace); R32 vs MLS: beat Austin FC 2-1 ON THE ROAD (Totsch 26', Showunmi 32')
louisville.stat_to_fear: Louisville has 18 goals across USL Championship and USL Cup this season — tied for MOST across Championship and League One (Opta)
louisville.the_lone_loss: Lost only once across all competitions in 2026.
louisville.history_at_stake: First trip to Open Cup Quarterfinals since 2018; would be FIRST EVER QF win on the road against an MLS team. They are 0W-4L-1D (lost on PKs at Seattle 2024) all-time visiting MLS in Open Cup.

1_the_loucity_takedown_tour.headline: Louisville already ate Austin. Houston could be next on the menu.
1_the_loucity_takedown_tour.details: Louisville beat Austin 2-1 in the R32 with two first-half goals (Totsch 26', Showunmi 32') — and did it at Q2 Stadium, the same place Houston just lost 2-0. So Austin lost to Louisville and beat Houston in a 4-day span. The transitive property says we should be terrified.
1_the_loucity_takedown_tour.added_spice: Louisville is on a three-game winning streak. Houston's three-game winning streak just ended in Austin. Vibes are inversely correlated.

2_pOY_vs_POY.headline: Reigning USL Players of the Year on opposite sides
2_pOY_vs_POY.details: Houston's NICK MARKANICH was the 2024 USL Championship Player of the Year (28 goals at Charleston Battery, all-time single-season record). Louisville's TAYLOR DAVILA is the 2025 USL Championship Player of the Year. Both former USL royalty. Markanich just scored his first Dynamo goal in the El Paso Open Cup win.
2_pOY_vs_POY.girlfriend_guide_take: Two USL exes meeting at the Cup. Markanich left for La Liga (Castellón loan to Houston) and is grinding back into MLS form. Davila is still the maestro in midfield. This is the central nervous system of the match.

3_the_showunmi_problem.headline: The Open Cup's leading scorer is in town
3_the_showunmi_problem.details: Tola Showunmi (25, English) leads the ENTIRE U.S. Open Cup in scoring with 4 goals (brace vs Southern Indiana, single vs Lexington, then the Austin dagger). He's also Louisville's top scorer in all comps with 5 goals.
3_the_showunmi_problem.his_winner_vs_austin: Voted Open Cup Goal of the Round.

4_the_homecoming_kid.headline: Louisville's captain almost was a Dynamo
4_the_homecoming_kid.details: Kyle Adams (29, New Zealand international, captain) was signed by Houston Dynamo organization in January 2020. He spent the season on loan with Rio Grande Valley FC Toros. Never broke through to the MLS roster. Now he's coming back wearing the captain's armband for the team trying to knock Houston out.
4_the_homecoming_kid.extra: He's listed as 'plant-powered athlete' in his IG bio. Sober king of the back line. First LouCity goal AND first LouCity assist this season.

5_lingr_back_olsen_rotates.headline: Lingr available; Olsen will likely shake the lineup
5_lingr_back_olsen_rotates.details: Ondřej Lingr's red card (vs SD on 4/22) was an MLS suspension — he served it against Austin. He's eligible for Open Cup. Expect heavy rotation: Aliyu, Ponce, Markanich, Bogusz, Resch, Vines, Maurer all featured in the El Paso win and could return. Sam Vines made first Dynamo start vs El Paso. Resch first Dynamo start vs El Paso.
5_lingr_back_olsen_rotates.olsen_open_cup_quote: Olsen on Open Cup (US Soccer): 'You survive long enough and you start to smell an opportunity to put up a Cup and that's a rare thing you don't want to miss out on.' He calls himself 'romantic about the Open Cup.'

6_simon_bird_interim_era.headline: Louisville's interim head coach has been here all along
6_simon_bird_interim_era.details: Simon Bird (English, U of Louisville Conference USA Player of the Year back in the day) took over as interim HC in March 2026 when Danny Cruz left for Minnesota United (assistant gig). Bird had been Cruz's top assistant for years. His first big test is right now.
6_simon_bird_interim_era.bird_quote_on_the_match: 'It's a difficult game for a number of reasons — one, [we] don't know a lot about the opponent, that's always difficult... this is one where really it's more about us and our process and our standards.'
6_simon_bird_interim_era.bird_on_being_banged_up: 'From a personnel standpoint we're a little banged up in certain areas but it also provides opportunity for players.' Translation: cup squad rotation possible on his side too.

starting_xi.name: Jimmy Maurer
starting_xi.note: Got the El Paso start, expect cup keeper again

starting_xi.name: Agustín Resch
starting_xi.note: First Dynamo start vs El Paso

starting_xi.name: Sam Vines
starting_xi.note: First Dynamo start vs El Paso

starting_xi.name: Mateusz Bogusz
starting_xi.note: 2024 LAFC cup winner; goal 22 sec into 2H vs El Paso

starting_xi.name: Nick Markanich
starting_xi.note: 2024 USL POY — point of the spear vs Davila

houston_likely_xi_open_cup.ponce_aliyu_note: Either could rotate in for Lingr or Bogusz — Ponce scored vs El Paso, Aliyu scored the SD winner.

starting_xi_likely.name: Brandon Dayes
starting_xi_likely.note: Started vs Austin, scored in USL match Apr 10

starting_xi_likely.name: Sean Totsch
starting_xi_likely.note: Goal vs Austin (26'), all-time USL regular-season appearances leader

starting_xi_likely.name: Taylor Davila
starting_xi_likely.note: 2025 USL POY; 4A across all comps, 7 Big Chances Created (T-USLC lead)

starting_xi_likely.name: Zach Duncan
starting_xi_likely.note: Started over Niang vs Austin, 11 shots from MF this year

starting_xi_likely.name: Ray Serrano
starting_xi_likely.note: Former Seattle Sounders Academy product, drift-into-space creator

starting_xi_likely.name: Evan Davila
starting_xi_likely.note: Taylor's younger brother — first goal of 2026 on April 10, started vs Austin

starting_xi_likely.name: Tola Showunmi
starting_xi_likely.note: Open Cup leading scorer (4 goals); team-leading 5 goals all comps

tola_showunmi.ig_bio_snippet: Tola Showunmi - Director (yes, his bio says Director). Moved to US for college soccer, graduated 2022 from University of New Hampshire with BSc.
tola_showunmi.stats_2026: 5 goals all comps (team lead); 4 Open Cup goals (TOURNAMENT LEAD); brace vs Southern Indiana, single vs Lexington, dagger vs Austin
tola_showunmi.the_tea: Open Cup leading scorer. His Austin winner was voted Open Cup Goal of the Round. Houston's defense will see his face in their dreams tonight.

taylor_davila.background: Cal Bears alum, former RGV FC midfielder
taylor_davila.honors: 2025 USL Championship PLAYER OF THE YEAR + All-League First Team
taylor_davila.stats_2026: 4 assists across all comps; 7 Big Chances Created (T-USL Championship lead)
taylor_davila.the_tea: Reigning USL POY. 'NO DEBATE' according to the league's IG. Calm, intelligent playmaker who picks the right pass and doesn't miss. He has a younger brother Evan on the same team — sibling DPs of vibes.

kyle_adams.stats_2026: First LouCity goal AND first LouCity assist this season
kyle_adams.the_homecoming: Houston Dynamo signed him in January 2020, sent him on loan to RGV Toros, never made it to MLS roster. Now he's the captain trying to ruin our night.
kyle_adams.girlfriend_guide_take: Vegan captain energy. Plant-powered armband. Coming home to seek revenge for his abandoned MLS dream.

sean_totsch.honor: USL all-time leader in regular season appearances
sean_totsch.stats_2026: Goal vs Austin (26' — opening goal of the upset)
sean_totsch.the_tea: Three-time USL Cup champion. Scratch golfer. His wife is @ayslien — they're high school sweethearts vibes; he posts birthday and anniversary tributes regularly. Wholesome center-back energy.

ray_serrano.background: Former Seattle Sounders Academy product; just hit 10,000 USL Championship minutes (April 18 milestone)
ray_serrano.stats_2026: Goal vs Fort Wayne in USL Cup; T-3 assists for Louisville this season
ray_serrano.role: Attacker — drifts into space, combines, creative complement to Showunmi
ray_serrano.the_tea: Young, slick, fluid. The kind of attacker who makes the highlights even when he doesn't score.

sean_totsch_wife_aysia.the_tea: Sean has been with his wife Aysia for years. He posts annual anniversary tributes ('Thank you for making life fun and full of color') and birthday tributes ('You bring passion, love and color to this life'). Local realtor friends call them 'an absolute pleasure to work with.' Wholesome high-school-sweethearts energy. He's a scratch golfer. They are the suburban dream couple.

taylor_evan_davila_brothers.the_angle: Taylor (2025 USL POY) and Evan Davila are BROTHERS playing on the same team. Evan, 24, finally scored his first goal of 2026 (and took his first shot of the season) on April 10. Imagine being the slightly-less-decorated younger brother to the league MVP. Sibling tension gold.

kyle_adams_plant_power.the_angle: Vegan captain. NZ international. Posts wholesome anniversary content. Houston org passed on him 6 years ago — petty revenge tour potential. His IG is @kyleadamsnz.

houston_side_updates.lingr_available: MLS suspension served against Austin. ELIGIBLE for Open Cup. Expect to start.
houston_side_updates.mcglynn_status: Returned from lower-body injury vs Austin (35 min off bench, 2 chances created, 4 crosses). Per Olsen 'day-to-day' but trending up.
el_paso_cup_starters_likely_back: Jimmy Maurer (GK — first start of year)
Aliyu Ibrahim (first start of year, 2023 cup winner)
Nick Markanich (first goal AND first start)
houston_side_updates.olsen_psychological_state: Olsen has won the Open Cup TWICE as a head coach (2013 DC, 2023 Houston) and once as a player (2008 DC). He's the third coach ever to lift it with two different clubs. He calls himself 'romantic about the Open Cup.' He WANTS this trophy.

match_outlook.houston_path_to_winning: Set the press, win the second balls, ride Lingr/Bogusz/Markanich into transition. Don't let Davila pick the lock. Show Showunmi he's not going to bully Sviatchenko/Negri.
match_outlook.louisville_path_to_winning: Same blueprint that beat Austin: get a goal early, force Houston to chase the game, let Showunmi do Showunmi things, ride Davila's tempo control into the night.
match_outlook.the_chaos_factor: Open Cup midweek + heavy rotations + USL champs on a winning streak vs MLS team coming off a derby loss. Nobody has done their best work this past week. Coin flip with extra-time energy.
match_outlook.predicted_score: Houston 2-1 Louisville (extra time vibes). Lingr ends his suspension nightmare with the winner. Showunmi scores the equalizer. Markanich scores the dagger. Olsen lifts an imaginary trophy.

context_for_the_guide.site_post_title_idea: Open Cup Tea: Louisville Already Ate Austin
context_for_the_guide.main_card_angle: USL royalty vs MLS pride — Reigning POY (Davila) vs former POY (Markanich) + the Open Cup's hottest striker (Showunmi)
context_for_the_guide.previous_match_recap_note: We dropped 2-0 at Austin on Saturday. Owen Wolff did NOT debut. Nelson and Uzuni scored. We move.
